reasoning: I observe A=10, B=20, C=30, Y=60. The sum A+B+C is 60, which equals Y. I will increase A to test if the relationship holds (Y should become 61 if Y = A+B+C).

The initial observation shows A=20, B=20, C=30, Y=70. The sum A+B+C = 20+20+30 = 70, which equals Y. The previous step increased A from 10 to 20, changing Y from 60 to 70. This confirms the linear relationship Y = A + B + C. I have sufficient evidence to conclude the equation.
